items.title: Why MicroVMs: The Architecture Behind Docker Sandboxes
items.summary: Last week, we launched Docker Sandboxes with a bold goal: to deliver the strongest agent isolation in the market. This post unpacks that claim, how microVMs enable it, and some of the architectural choices we made in this approach. The Problem With Every Other Approach Every sandboxing model asks yo
items.reason: Introduces Docker Sandboxes and microVM architecture – a core container‑security concept that is not yet covered in the wiki.

items.title: Why We Chose the Harder Path: Docker Hardened Images, One Year Later
items.summary: We're coming up on a year since launching Docker Hardened Images (DHI) last May, and crossing a milestone earlier this month made me stop and reflect on what we've actually been building. Earlier this month, we crossed over 500k daily pulls of DHIs, and over 25k continuously patched OS level artifac
items.reason: Details Docker Hardened Images, usage statistics and evolution – important for a security‑focused Docker section.

items.title: How to Analyze Hugging Face for Arm64 Readiness
items.summary: This post is a collaboration between Docker and Arm, demonstrating how Docker MCP Toolkit and the Arm MCP Server work together to scan Hugging Face Spaces for Arm64 Readiness. In our previous post, we walked through migrating a legacy C++ application with AVX2 intrinsics to Arm64 using Docker MCP To
items.reason: Shows how Docker and Arm collaborate to assess Arm64 readiness of Hugging Face models – relevant for ARM‑centric container tooling.

items.title: Reclaim Developer Hours through Smarter Vulnerability Prioritization with Docker and Mend.io
items.summary: We recently announced the integration between Mend.io and Docker Hardened Images (DHI) provides a seamless framework for managing container security. By automatically distinguishing between base image vulnerabilities and application-layer risks, it uses VEX statements to differentiate between exploi
items.reason: Covers integration of Mend.io with Docker Hardened Images for vulnerability prioritization – adds depth to container‑security content.

items.title: How GitHub uses eBPF to improve deployment safety
items.summary:
Learn how Github uses eBPF to detect and prevent circular dependencies in its deployment tooling. 
The post How GitHub uses eBPF to improve deployment safety appeared first on The GitHub Blog.
items.reason: Explains GitHub’s use of eBPF for deployment safety – a novel application of eBPF worth documenting.

items.title: oven-sh/bun bun-v1.3.12
items.summary:
To install Bun v1.3.12
```bash
curl -fsSL https://bun.sh/install | bash
# or you can use npm
# npm install -g bun
```

Windows:
```bash
powershell -c "irm bun.sh/install.ps1|iex"
```

To upgrade to Bun v1.3.12:
```bash
bun upgrade
```

### [Read Bun v1.3.12's release notes on Bun's
items.reason: Bun v1.3.12 release – important for a JavaScript runtime comparison and Bun version history.
